hero_subtitle: SFTY supports institutional teams with role-based access, audit logs, evidence integrity and governance to prepare on-premise compliance deployments for scalable, secure operations.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Audit logs need stronger deployment control
problem_description: Enterprise compliance teams struggle to maintain clear governance and evidence integrity without tightly managed on-premise deployment controls. This creates challenges in audit readiness, role-based access, and consistent evidence management across complex institutional environments.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY centralizes role-based access, audit logs and evidence integrity into one controlled on-premise environment, enabling institutional teams to manage governance and maintain audit-ready compliance with consistent operational oversight.
workflow_title: From deployment setup to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY structures on-premise deployment controls with role-based access, audit logs, evidence retention and governance to support institutional compliance readiness.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another deployment. Controlled enterprise readiness
why_sfty_description: SFTY provides regulated businesses a secured, role-based compliance layer that preserves audit evidence, enforces governance and supports on-premise operational control without replacing existing tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports regulated VASPs with provider-neutral orchestration, audit-ready evidence and bank due diligence for secure, scalable on-premise enterprise compliance deployments.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support on-premise deployment readiness
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how role-based access supports compliance governance
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit logs preserve evidence integrity on-premise
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY supports bank due diligence packages
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for enterprise compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer connecting KYC, KYT, Travel Rule, screening, case management, reporting and audit evidence into structured workflows for regulated digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support on-premise deployment?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports on-premise deployment with role-based access, audit logs, secure governance, configurable permissions and data retention suited for institutional compliance environments.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit evidence through decision logs, case history, regulatory rule change tracking and structured evidence packages for review and inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect our existing provider stack?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates existing compliance providers without replacing them, turning their outputs into connected cases, workflows, decisions and reports.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace compliance or KYC providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC or compliance providers but connects their outputs into an operational, auditable compliance layer.
final_title: Review on-premise deployment readiness with SFTY
final_description: Explore how SFTY supports role-based access, audit logging, evidence integrity and governance for institutional VASP deployments.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
